app.name: AwesomeAssertions
app.one_liner: A community-controlled fork of FluentAssertions that preserves open licensing while adding modern .NET support and enhanced assertion capabilities.

screens.name: Assertion Builder
screens.purpose: Allows developers to construct and execute fluent assertions against test subjects.

rust_commands.name: save_item
rust_commands.purpose: Persist assertion configuration or test result metadata for debugging or reporting.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add support for numerical approximate equality
week 1: Configure CI pipeline with GitHub Actions and coverage reporting
week 1: Implement core fluent assertion API compatible with FluentAssertions v7.x
week 1: Integrate with MSTest V4 and NUnit test runners
acceptance_tests: Given a collection of floating-point numbers, when asserting approximate equality with tolerance 0.01, then the test passes if all elements are within tolerance.
Given a test project using FluentAssertions 7.0.0, when applying the Renovate replacement rule, then the build succeeds and all existing assertions behave identically.
